feedback.text: Love that you have a full vegan menu! Most pizza places just throw one option on the menu as an afterthought. The separate experience feels premium.
The website looks nice but I couldn't figure out how to actually order online. Ended up calling instead.
The map on your site is really handy! Found you easily on my first visit. Nice touch with the popup showing the address.
feedback.related_feature: Add interactive map on Find Us page (#16)

feedback.text: Customer at table 4 asked if we have photos of the pizzas on the website. She wanted to show her friend what to expect. I told her not yet.
Tried to check the menu on my phone while on the bus but had no signal. Would be nice if the site worked offline somehow.
My Danish isn't great so I really appreciate the English option on the website! Made it so easy to read the menu before coming in.
Do you guys have any deals or a pizza of the week? Would love something to look forward to each time I visit your site.
Group of tourists at table 7 couldn't navigate the site on their phones. They said the menu was hard to tap and the nav was confusing on a small screen.
Charming story on the About page. Love learning about the family history. Made me feel connected to the place before I even walked in.
feedback.related_feature: Create a page with the history of the pizzaria (#8)

feedback.text: Would be great if you had customer reviews on your website. I always check reviews before trying a new restaurant.
I have a nut allergy and couldn't tell which pizzas are safe from your menu. Had to call and ask. Please add allergen info!
Regular customer Marco mentioned he checks the site every week. Said the vegan section convinced his daughter to try us — she's now a regular too.
Nice website but there's no way to contact you other than calling. Would be handy to have a contact form for questions about catering.
The site was hard to read at night on my phone. Too bright. A dark mode would be appreciated.
Just discovered you can switch between classic and vegan! My partner and I always argue about this — now we can each browse our own menu. Genius.
A family with a wheelchair user struggled to find accessibility info on the site. They asked if we have step-free access and couldn't find it online.
When I share the link to friends on WhatsApp it just shows a plain URL with no preview image or description. Looks dodgy compared to other restaurants.
It would be fun if you had some kind of game or quiz on the site. My kids always grab my phone while we wait for the food.
The font is really clean and easy to read. Way better than those fancy cursive fonts other restaurants use that I can never decipher.
feedback.related_feature: Change the font styling to not use serifs (#5)

feedback.text: Overheard a couple at table 2 complaining they wanted to see what the pizzas actually look like. 'How can they not have photos?' they said.
Would love to know about events and live music ahead of time. Maybe an events page? I only found out about Friday open-mic by accident.
The directions on the Find Us page saying 5 mins from Central Station was spot on. Found you without Google Maps!
feedback.related_feature: Add interactive map on Find Us page (#16)

feedback.text: Do you deliver? I couldn't find any info about delivery zones on your site. We're just on Istedgade, would love to order from home.
Table 9 tried to print the menu from the website to bring to a party planning session. Said it came out looking awful with all the navigation and colors.
Cool name btw. Cool Pizza. Makes me smile every time. The whole site has a nice modern vibe without being pretentious.
feedback.related_feature: Change the name to Cool Pizza (#18)

feedback.text: My friend recommended this place but when I looked up 'Cool Pizza Copenhagen' nothing came up on Google. Only found it through the direct link she sent.
I'm visually impaired and use a screen reader. The site is mostly okay but some buttons don't have labels and the page changes don't announce properly.
The photo gallery is great! Finally I can show my colleagues what the pizzas look like. The truffle one looks amazing — definitely ordering that next time.
Multiple customers this week mentioned they saw the Pizza of the Week on the website before coming in. Two specifically asked for the Tartufo Bianco.
feedback.related_feature: Add Pizza of the Week highlight (#22)

feedback.text: Love that I can tap the hamburger menu now! Before it was impossible to navigate on my phone without accidentally tapping the wrong link. So much better.
The gallery photos are nice but they all look the same — just the pizza. Would love to see photos of the restaurant interior, the staff, or people enjoying the food.
Clicked through to your Instagram from the website. Great feed! Makes me want to follow and see what's cooking each day. Smart to put those links in the footer.
feedback.related_feature: Add social media links and Instagram feed (#29)

feedback.text: Customer asked why the Pizza of the Week hasn't changed in over a week. They expected it to update every Monday. We need a process for keeping this fresh.
feedback.related_feature: Add Pizza of the Week highlight (#22)

feedback.text: The testimonials on the home page convinced me to try you guys. When I saw that other people praised the vegan menu, I knew I had to check it out. Wasn't disappointed!
Tried to zoom in on a gallery photo on my phone to see the toppings more clearly but the lightbox image was still pretty small. Pinch to zoom would be nice.
The hamburger menu is fine but when I open it, I can still scroll the page behind it. Feels janky. Also the menu covers the language toggle on desktop when I resize my browser.
A couple at table 6 said they found us through the Instagram link on someone else's share of our site. The social links are driving word-of-mouth.
feedback.related_feature: Add social media links and Instagram feed (#29)

feedback.text: Could you add a way to share a specific gallery photo directly to Instagram stories? I wanted to show my friends the truffle pizza but had to screenshot instead.
The testimonials section only shows 5 reviews and they're all positive. Feels a bit fake honestly. Real restaurants have some mixed feedback too.
The dark mode is a lifesaver! I always check the menu late at night when planning tomorrow's lunch and the bright white screen used to blind me. Love it.
My daughter has a dairy allergy and the new filter on your menu is AMAZING. We could instantly see what's safe. First time we didn't have to ask the waiter about every single item.
feedback.related_feature: Add allergen & dietary filter on menu (#41)

feedback.text: The allergen filter is great but I wish there was an egg allergy option too. Some of the doughs use egg and I can't tell from the current tags.
feedback.related_feature: Add allergen & dietary filter on menu (#41)

feedback.text: Table 3 printed the menu from the website to plan their office pizza order. Said the printout looked clean and professional. They ordered 8 pizzas.
Found out about the Friday open-mic through your events page! Brought my guitar and had a blast. Please keep that page updated — I check it every week now.
I used the contact form to ask about catering last week but never got a reply. Is anyone actually reading those messages?
My kids spent 10 minutes on the pizza builder deciding what they want. It actually made the wait for a table fun instead of chaotic. Smart feature!
A customer at table 11 showed me the allergen filter on her phone. She said she's been to 3 other pizza places in Copenhagen and none have this. Called it 'how it should be everywhere'.
feedback.related_feature: Add allergen & dietary filter on menu (#41)

feedback.text: The events page is nice but I'd love to get a notification or email when new events are posted. I keep forgetting to check.
Tried to use the allergen filter for 'gluten free' options but literally everything disappeared except the drinks. Is there really nothing gluten-free to eat here?
feedback.related_feature: Add allergen & dietary filter on menu (#41)

feedback.text: The events page convinced my flatmates to come for the charity night. We ended up being 6 people who wouldn't have known about it otherwise. Great initiative!
Multiple customers this week asked if we could add their pizza to some kind of favourites list. They want to remember what they ordered last time.
Printed the menu to bring to a birthday party planning session. Everyone could see the options and vote. The fact that it came out looking nice (no website junk) was a big plus.
The pizza builder is fun but misleading — my kid thought she was actually ordering when she pressed buttons. Maybe make it clearer it's just for fun.
I use the nut filter every time but I'm never sure if 'may contain traces' is covered or just direct ingredients. Could you clarify that somewhere on the filter?
feedback.related_feature: Add allergen & dietary filter on menu (#41)

feedback.text: Just noticed the focus outlines when tabbing through your site! As someone who uses keyboard navigation due to RSI, this is really appreciated. Most restaurant sites completely ignore this.
I can finally close the photo viewer with Escape! Before I had to find the tiny X button. Small thing but makes a big difference when browsing on the laptop without a mouse.

Accessibility consultant from the council visited and tested the site. Said keyboard navigation is solid now but mentioned the contrast of some text in vegan mode could be improved for low-vision users.

top_themes_liked: Pizza of the Week driving orders
top_themes_didnt_like: Pizza of the Week not updating frequently
